candidates.reasoning: The combination of dense Loblolly Pine (Pinus taeda) plantations and mixed deciduous hardwoods is highly characteristic of the Southeastern United States. The combination of right-hand driving, a yellow outer edge line (fog line), and white dashed lane dividers is highly characteristic of US road standards. This specific marking configuration is distinct from most European countries which typically use white for all edge lines.

Canada shares many road infrastructure standards with the US, including right-hand driving and similar lane markings. However, yellow edge lines are less common across all provinces compared to the US.
